danger_signals.visual_form: White-colored entities and tiles appearing in player dungeon space
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy forces intent on stealing treasures, killing monsters, and destroying the player's Dungeon Heart

danger_signals.visual_form: Dungeon Heart structure being damaged or destroyed
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate loss condition; Dungeon Heart represents the Keeper's link to the world

danger_signals.visual_form: Behavioral changes in creature animation and movement patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Creatures will vandalize dungeon infrastructure and desert the player if angry

danger_signals.visual_form: Creatures not proceeding to Treasury on payday cycles
danger_signals.meaning: Creatures require regular payment; failure to pay leads to desertion and hostile behavior

danger_signals.visual_form: Special final hero entity (resembling Avatar from Ultima VIII: Pagan)
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat level; final boss-tier enemy force

safety_signals.meaning: Territory under player control where rooms and traps can be built

safety_signals.visual_form: New minions materializing at Portal entrance
safety_signals.meaning: Successful recruitment and reinforcement of dungeon forces

safety_signals.visual_form: Creatures gathering at Treasury on payday, gold/gem accumulation visual feedback
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability and creature morale maintenance

safety_signals.visual_form: Colored stars above creatures displaying experience level (color matches keeper faction)
safety_signals.meaning: Increasing combat capability and dungeon security
Ability to improve creature happiness and access sacrificial mechanics for rewards

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red-colored tiles, red experience stars above minions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Red-tinted owned territory; Portal entrance for minion recruitment; Workshop, Lair, Hatchery, Treasury, Temple infrastructure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different color-coded tiles and minion experience stars; minions can be captured or scavenged
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy dungeon infrastructure visible on map; colored tile claims indicate territorial possession

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: White tiles and white-colored entities within player space
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Invading from outside dungeon; moving toward treasure and Dungeon Heart; distinct from minion creatures through color and behavior
Neutral entities that can be recruited or converted through various mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Small red dungeon territory; basic rooms (Lair, Hatchery, Treasury) becoming visible as built
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Imps actively digging; few minions present via Portal; mentor voiceover providing guidance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Expanding red tile claims; room blueprints and incomplete structures; gold/gem seams being mined
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Imps prioritizing flagged dig locations; creatures training in Training Room; no immediate threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple specialized rooms built; Portal active with steady minion arrivals; colored experience stars appearing above creatures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Diverse creature types present (dragons, warlocks, horned reapers, undead); creatures performing role-specific tasks; payday cycles visible at Treasury

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unlocking of advanced rooms, spells, and trap types visible through construction menu and dungeon layout changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New room types becoming buildable; access to more powerful minion types; expanded tactical options

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy-colored tiles encroaching on player territory; rival minions visible at dungeon boundaries; colored experience stars indicating enemy forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat encounters between player and rival minions; territorial competition for dungeon space and resources

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: White tiles and white-colored hero entities appearing within player dungeon; heroes moving toward treasure and Dungeon Heart
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player minions engaging heroes in combat; creatures fighting to defend dungeon infrastructure; urgency in mentor voiceover

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Creatures refusing to work; visible vandalism in dungeon rooms; creatures moving away from work areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures deserting the player; dungeon infrastructure being sabotaged; decreased combat readiness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temple room showing sacrifice activity; potential visual effects indicating god reward or punishment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gods may reward player (positive effects on creatures/resources), punish (negative effects), or be indifferent based on sacrifice quality

environmental_storytelling.example: Red tiles = player territory; white tiles = hero-held space; other colors = rival keepers. Territory visually communicates control and sovereignty without UI overlay
Flies and Spiders naturally at odds; certain creatures refuse to perform certain tasks. Visual gameplay interactions show dungeon hierarchy and creature relationships

environmental_storytelling.technique: Room-based creature recruitment prerequisite visual gating
environmental_storytelling.example: Which minion types arrive via Portal depends on which rooms the player has built and their size. Dungeon infrastructure determines population composition visually

environmental_storytelling.technique: Experience star color matching faction ownership
environmental_storytelling.example: Colored stars above creatures match keeper faction color. Visual indicator of creature allegiance and experience level simultaneously
Creatures actively migrating to Treasury on payday cycles. Visual behavior demonstrates economy-driven creature morale system
Sinister voiceover provides narrative framing of dungeon events. Audio-visual combo establishes dark, comedic tone and world personality

environmental_storytelling.technique: Trap and door placement visual feedback
environmental_storytelling.example: Blueprints must be physically placed by Imps; not instant. Visual delay communicates construction time and resource commitment
Slapping creatures forces faster work but removes health; visible creature damage state reflects dungeon master's harsh treatment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Actively digging tunnels, capturing rooms, mining gold/gems, setting traps on flagged tiles
Temporarily accelerate work speed but sustain health loss
Lowest experience level; require training in Training Room to gain attributes and spells
Head directly to Treasury to collect wages; absence indicates economic crisis
Vandalize dungeon infrastructure; desert the player; refuse work orders
Engage heroes or rival keeper minions; behavior varies by creature type and training level
Some excel at specific tasks; others refuse to perform certain tasks based on creature type restrictions
Move through dungeon toward treasure and Dungeon Heart with intent to steal and destroy
Ultimate threat; final boss-tier invasion wave
If destroyed, player loses immediately; central system failure condition

notes: Dungeon Keeper uses a color-coded tile system as primary faction/ownership indicator, eliminating ambiguity in territory control. The hand cursor (Hand of Evil) serves as player avatar and primary interaction metaphor, communicating direct dungeon master authority. Dark humor tone is reinforced through sinister mentor voiceover and creature ecology design. Economic system (payday cycles, gold/gem accumulation) is visually manifested through creature behavior patterns. Creature experience stars displaying faction color create dual-information visual feedback (allegiance + power level).
